name: kneeling push-up (male)
instructions: Start by kneeling on the ground with your hands shoulder-width apart, fingers pointing forward.
Extend your legs behind you, resting on the balls of your feet, so that your body forms a straight line from head to heels.
Engage your core and lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides.
Continue lowering until your chest is just above the ground, then push back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Start by kneeling on the ground with your hands shoulder-width apart, fingers pointing forward. Extend your legs behind you, resting on the balls of your feet, so that your body forms a straight line from head to heels. Engage your core and lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides. Continue lowering until your chest is just above the ground, then push back up to the starting position by straightening your arms. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
